Title: Harcourt-Smith, Cecil, Sir
Overview: Director of the Victoria and Albert Museum, archaeologist. Early on he encouraged the son of a family friend, Trenchard Cox, then a Cambridge student, to seriously consider art museum work (Cox later became a director of the V&A). He retired in 1924 and was succeeded by Eric Maclagan.
Sources: The Makers of Classical Archaeology: A Reference Work. New York: Humanity Books, 2000 pp. 141-42.
